title: MGL c.121B s.32 - Public housing maintenance, operation, tenant selection and evictions
content:
Section 32: Maintenance and operation of project; rentals; tenant selection; eligibility for continued occupancy; hearings; waiver
Section 32. Upon the completion or acquisition of a housing project by a housing authority, it shall be maintained and operated by such authority. It is hereby declared to be the policy of this commonwealth that each housing authority shall manage and operate decent, safe and sanitary dwelling accommodations at the lowest possible cost, and that no housing authority shall manage and operate any such project for profit.

Rent Calculation:
An authority shall fix the rents for dwelling units in its projects in accordance with regulations issued by the department, so that no tenant shall be required to pay a rental of more than 32 percent of his income if heat, cooking fuel and electricity are provided by the authority, 30 percent of his income if one or more utility is provided, or 27 percent of his income if such utilities are not provided. In no instance shall a tenant household pay a rental fee of less than $5 per household, provided that exceptions to payment of such minimum rent shall be allowed in accordance with regulations issued by the department.

Hardship Exemption:
An authority shall grant an exemption from application of the minimum monthly rent to any resident unable to pay such amount because of severe financial hardship, which shall include situations in which the family is awaiting an eligibility determination for an application for any federal, state, or local assistance program, the tenant would be evicted as a result of the imposition of the minimum rent requirements, the income of the tenant has decreased because of changed circumstances, including involuntary loss of employment, the occurrence of a death in the household, and such other severe financial hardship situations as may be determined by the housing authority.

Tenant Selection Priorities:
In the operation or management of state-aided low-rent housing projects an authority shall observe the following requirements:
(a) It shall rent or lease the dwelling accommodations therein only at rentals within the financial reach of persons and families of low income.
(b) It shall rent to a tenant dwelling accommodations consisting of the least number of rooms necessary to provide safe and sanitary accommodations without overcrowding.
(e) There shall be no discrimination or segregation; provided, that if the number of qualified applicants exceed the dwelling units available, preference shall be given to inhabitants of the city or town in which the project is located.
(f) As between applicants equally in need and eligible, preference shall be given: (1) to families displaced by any low-rent housing project or public improvement, with first preference to families of disabled veterans; (2) to families of other veterans; and (3) to persons displaced by other public action.

Termination of Tenancy:
The tenancy of a tenant of a housing authority shall not be terminated without cause and without reasons therefor given to said tenant in writing prior to filing an action for summary process. A tenant at his request shall be granted a hearing by a housing authority at least fifteen days prior to any such termination, except in the case of non-payment of rent, or if there is reason to believe that the tenant or a member of the tenant's household has: (1) unlawfully caused serious physical harm to another; (2) threatened to seriously physically harm another; (3) destroyed, vandalized or stolen property creating a serious threat to health or safety; (4) possessed or illegally kept a weapon; (5) unlawfully possessed, sold, or possessed with intent to distribute a controlled substance; (6) engaged in other criminal conduct which seriously threatened or endangered health or safety; (7) for any of the reasons set forth in section nineteen of chapter one hundred and thirty-nine; or (8) a guest engages in any such behavior where the tenant knew or should have known.

In the event the housing authority brings an action for summary process, such action shall be accorded an expedited hearing and trial if any of the above reasons are alleged. If the court enters judgment for possession in favor of the housing authority on account of one or more of the above reasons, the court's judgment shall not be stayed pending any appeal unless the court makes written findings that there is a reasonable likelihood that the tenant will prevail on appeal.
